commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=ee3c5ff349c8027fbb1120828fefe4c3fc4417cc)
commands.review_summary:
The PR lowers the read tool’s automatic page budget for known small model context windows, enforces capped read output with continuation notices, and adds focused read-tool regressions.

PR surface: Source +138, Tests +135. Total +273 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the central behavior by source inspection: current main clamps known 16k-context reads to the 32KB floor, while the PR lowers that known-context floor and caps output at the smaller budget. I did not run the focused tests in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - The remaining merge decision is maintainer acceptance of a compatibility-sensitive implicit default change; the small fidelity finding is mechanical but not the main blocker.

target.title: fix(agents): scale read output for small contexts
target.body:
Summary:
- Scale the default read-tool cap down for models with small advertised context windows while preserving the existing 32KB default when no context is known.
- Keep explicit `read(limit=...)` calls authoritative, and avoid trusting continuation-looking file text as metadata.
- Keep capped read output inside the byte budget with usable continuation guidance for short-line files, long-line files, over-budget first lines, and first lines that need the whole cap.

Verification:
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/agent-tools.create-openclaw-coding-tools.adds-claude-style-aliases-schemas-without-dropping-g.test.ts` (2 files, 26 tests)
- `git diff --check $(git merge-base HEAD origin/main)..HEAD`
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/agents/agent-tools.read.ts src/agents/agent-tools.create-openclaw-coding-tools.adds-claude-style-aliases-schemas-without-dropping-g.test.ts`
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxlint src/agents/agent-tools.read.ts src/agents/agent-tools.create-openclaw-coding-tools.adds-claude-style-aliases-schemas-without-dropping-g.test.ts`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main` (clean)
- `node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --idle-timeout 45m --ttl 90m --shell -- "node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/agent-tools.create-openclaw-coding-tools.adds-claude-style-aliases-schemas-without-dropping-g.test.ts"` (AWS Crabbox `cbx_1245f584b00f`, run `run_3bbaa2fbb3ae`, 2 files, 26 tests)

Behavior addressed: small/local model turns can lose too much prompt budget to default file reads even though the runner already knows the model context window.
Real environment tested: AWS Crabbox Linux runner, provider `aws`, lease `cbx_1245f584b00f`, slug `pearl-barnacle`, run `run_3bbaa2fbb3ae`, exact pushed head `ee3c5ff349c8027fbb1120828fefe4c3fc4417cc`, rebased onto `origin/main` at `2be1d1b9f98df86965788201b61117210e371220`.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: ran the focused read-tool regression file locally and remotely after rebasing; static checks covered diff whitespace, formatting, and lint on the two touched files.
Evidence after fix: focused Vitest reported 2 files and 26 tests passed locally and on AWS Crabbox; autoreview reported no accepted/actionable findings.
Observed result after fix: small-context reads cap near 8KB, preserve continuation guidance that can make progress, do not parse continuation-looking file content as metadata, and keep the cap notice within the byte budget.
What was not tested: full repo check; this PR only touches `src/agents/agent-tools.read.ts` and its focused regression test.

Related: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/86599
target.author: vincentkoc
